title: strychnos soubrensis
intro: Strychnos soubrensis, also known as the yellow wood or yellow strychnine tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Loganiaceae family. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a stimulant and a purgative, and has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Strychnos soubrensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by drying the bark or seeds, and then using them in decoctions, infusions, or as a powder for medicinal purposes.
